stories.title: Michigan bills aim to shield residents from subsidizing data center utility costs
stories.dek: Lawmakers introduced legislation requiring data centers to meet standards on power use, water consumption and workforce practices. The package aims to prevent residents from subsidizing the industry's growth through higher utility bills. Passage would reshape how future campuses are sited across the state.
stories.source_title: Michigan Data Center Legislation: Energy, Water & Labor Rules - MultiState

stories.title: Warren council pushes one-year halt on new data centers
stories.dek: Warren's city council voted to seek a moratorium on data center proposals amid worries over power demand and noise. The move would give local officials time to write zoning rules before any projects advance. Macomb County residents now watch to see if neighboring communities follow.
stories.source_title: Warren city council calls for data center moratorium - Macomb Daily

stories.title: Freedom Township residents block billion-dollar data center push
stories.dek: Locals in the Washtenaw County township organized to stop a massive project that would have consumed huge amounts of power and water. Their successful push shows how small communities can alter the trajectory of AI-driven builds. Property values and township services stay protected for now.
stories.source_title: Fortitude: How Freedom Township Found Its Voice in a Billion-Dollar Fight - The Sun Times News

stories.title: Whitmer urges data centers to sign pledge shielding ratepayers
stories.dek: The governor called on developers to commit to protecting Michigan residents from higher utility bills and strained water supplies. The voluntary plan comes as multiple townships weigh their own restrictions on new projects. Statewide adoption could shape how billions in new investment affect local communities.
stories.source_title: Whitmer calls on data centers to protect residents, resources - Detroit Free Press

stories.title: Wixom keeps data center moratorium after rejecting waiver request
stories.dek: Wixom city council turned down a waiver that would have allowed a data center project to move forward. The moratorium remains in effect, blocking new proposals while the city studies impacts on power and local infrastructure. Residents in this Oakland County suburb get more time before any large-scale facilities can be approved.
stories.source_title: Wixom city council rejects data center waiver, moratorium stays in place - MLive.com

stories.title: Solon Township shelves rules aimed at limiting data centers
stories.dek: The board postponed an ordinance that would have restricted where large facilities could locate in Kent County. Residents had pressed for tighter controls over water and electricity demand. The delay keeps current zoning in place while debate continues.
stories.source_title: Solon Township board tables ordinance proposal to limit data centers - AOL.com

stories.title: Lenox Township rumors trigger recall push against board
stories.dek: Residents in Lenox Township are circulating recall petitions after the board delayed action on rumored data-center proposals. The delay leaves open questions about how much power and water a large facility would pull from local supplies. Voters could face a recall election that decides who controls future zoning decisions.
stories.source_title: Lenox Township board tables review as data center rumors spark recall effort - MLive.com

stories.title: Mason County data center site floods again, alarming nearby residents
stories.dek: The construction site in Mason County flooded for a second time, prompting fresh concerns from locals about runoff, road damage and long-term site stability. Residents worry repeated flooding could affect nearby properties and water quality. County officials have not yet detailed any fixes or new drainage requirements.
stories.source_title: Mason County data center construction site floods again; locals concerned - Charleston Gazette-Mail

stories.title: Mason mayor survives recall attempt as county clerk tosses signatures
stories.dek: Ingham County Clerk rejected signature petitions aimed at recalling Mason’s mayor over his data-center stance. The decision keeps the mayor in office and blocks a special election residents had sought. Supporters say they will regroup and refile, keeping the fight over local data-center policy alive.
stories.source_title: Ingham County Clerk rejects Mason mayor recall petition - AOL.com

stories.title: Marysville eyes one-year freeze on new data centers
stories.dek: City leaders want to pause proposals while they study power and water demands. Residents 30 minutes away could see higher utility bills if large projects move in unchecked. The move would give local officials time to write zoning rules before any deals are signed.
stories.source_title: City wants data center moratorium - The Marysville Advocate

stories.title: Michigan School Sues Over Noise From Candidate's Bitcoin Data Center
stories.dek: Lake Superior Academy recorded up to 72.7 decibels from the six shipping-container operation next door. Michael Carbonara, named in the suit, is running for Congress in Florida's Project Tango district.

national.title: Data centers advance rapidly as regulators scramble for rules
national.dek: Across the U.S., officials race to impose guardrails on power-hungry facilities while communities push back on incentives and impacts.
